Language Certification

NAT-TEST

Nihongo Ability Test

The NAT-TEST is a flexible and widely accepted Japanese proficiency certification, held 6 times per year. With more test dates than JLPT, it's an excellent choice for those needing faster certification turnaround.

Enroll NowAsk a Question
📝
LevelsN5 → N1
Duration2–10 Months
Exams/Year6 Times
FormatOffline Test
Reserve Your Spot
What is NAT-TEST?

About the Test

The Nihongo Ability Test (NAT-TEST) is administered by the NAT-TEST Center in Japan. It evaluates Japanese language ability across five levels (N1–N5) using four skills: vocabulary, grammar, reading, and listening.

One of NAT-TEST's biggest advantages over JLPT is its frequency — offered 6 times per year (every odd month), giving learners more opportunities to attempt and pass the exam quickly.

It is recognized by many Japanese employers and immigration offices as a valid proof of Japanese ability, especially at N3–N1 levels.

NAT-TEST Levels

N5 (5級)

Most basic level — ideal for complete beginners.

N4 (4級)

Elementary Japanese for familiar and everyday situations.

N3 (3級)

Intermediate — daily conversations and basic workplace Japanese.

N2 (2級)

Upper intermediate — used in formal and professional settings.

N1 (1級)

Advanced — complex language for academic and business use.

📅 Test held every odd month (Jan, Mar, May, Jul, Sep, Nov).
Comparison

NAT-TEST vs JLPT

Test Frequency
NAT: 6 times/year
JLPT: 2 times/year
Levels
NAT: N1–N5
JLPT: N1–N5
Recognition
NAT: Widely accepted
JLPT: Globally standard
Difficulty
NAT: Slightly easier access
JLPT: More rigorous scoring
Best For
NAT: Quick certification
JLPT: International prestige
What We Cover

Course Curriculum

Vocabulary & Kanji

Word Foundation

  • Level-specific vocabulary sets
  • Kanji reading and writing
  • Word usage in context
  • Mnemonics and memory tools
Grammar & Reading

Language Structure

  • All grammar points by level
  • Reading comprehension passages
  • Formal vs informal language
  • Passage analysis techniques
Listening & Mock Tests

Exam Readiness

  • Authentic listening exercises
  • Speed reading practice
  • 4 full-length mock tests
  • Answer strategy coaching
Batches

Class Schedule

📋
N5/N4 Morning
Monday – Friday
6:00 AM – 8:00 AM
📋
N3/N2 Afternoon
Monday – Friday
3:00 PM – 5:00 PM
📋
N1 Saturday
Saturday
8:00 AM – 12:00 PM
